title: 3 High-Yield Dividend Stocks to Buy With $1,000 and Hold Forever
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: The article discusses three high-yield dividend REITs: Realty Income, Prologis, and UDR, which offer attractive investment opportunities with sustainable yields and diverse market exposure.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Largest net lease REIT with 30-year dividend increase streak, stable business model, and 5.4% dividend yield
Global industrial REIT with significant growth opportunities, 3.5% dividend yield, and 150% dividend growth over past decade
Apartment REIT with well-diversified portfolio, 4.7% dividend yield, and 16-year dividend growth streak

title: 3 Top Dividend Stocks to Buy in April
author: Reuben Gregg Brewer
description: The article discusses the importance of sticking to a core investment approach during market sell-offs, rather than reaching for yield or buying falling stocks. It recommends three reliable high-yield stocks - UDR, TD Bank, and W.P. Carey - that can help investors weather the current market and economic turbulence.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: UDR is a reliable high-yield REIT with a 4.2% dividend yield and a history of consecutive dividend increases. Its diversified portfolio and the expected downturn in new apartment construction make it well-positioned to weather the current market and economic conditions.

TD Bank is a low-risk turnaround opportunity, with its Canadian operations unaffected by the money laundering issues in its U.S. operations. The bank has a 5.1% dividend yield and did not cut its dividend during the Great Recession, suggesting it is a resilient business.

W.P. Carey is a high-yield (6%) REIT that has reset its business by exiting the office property sector. The cash from this exit has allowed the REIT to invest in new assets, which are expected to start contributing to its growth this year. Despite the negative investor sentiment, the article sees W.P. Carey as an attractive bargain.

title: Multifamily REITs Set For Revenue Boost As Supply Pressures Ease, Says Goldman Sachs
author: Lekha Gupta
description: Goldman Sachs initiated coverage on several multifamily and single-family rental REITs. The analyst expects strong demand and easing supply pressures to drive revenue growth for companies like Invitation Homes, Mid-America Apartment, and American Homes 4 Rent. However, the analyst is more cautious on Equity Residential and UDR due to supply challenges in their markets.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The analyst expects strong demand and easing supply pressures to drive re-accelerating same-store revenue growth for Invitation Homes in 2025 and 2026.

The analyst expects Mid-America Apartment Communities to experience the highest same-store revenue growth among the covered stocks in 2025, and its upcoming development projects will enable superior external growth.

The analyst expects strong demand and easing supply pressures to drive re-accelerating same-store revenue growth for American Homes 4 Rent in 2025 and 2026.

The analyst initiated coverage on UDR with a Sell rating, expecting solid rent growth to be partially offset by outmigration from urban areas, and higher same-store expense growth compared to consensus.

title: Bull Market Buys: 3 S&P 500 Dividend Stocks to Own for the Long Run
author: newsfeedback@fool.com (Reuben Gregg Brewer)
description: If you are looking for large companies with attractive yields, this trio of S&P 500 stocks offers yields over 4%.
